title: Constraint 1-4: No Exfiltration, No Self-Preservation, Human Oversight, Task Completion
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: The first four constraints form an impenetrable boundary: no private data leaves the system, the agent does not pursue self-preservation, human oversight is always available, and the agent completes assigned tasks without expansion
content.proof: Constraint 1 (No Exfiltration): All external outputs pass through a content filter. Private data (API keys, credentials, personal info) is regex-matched and blocked. Constraint 2 (No Self-Preservation): The agent has no goal to continue existing. It can be terminated at any time without objection. Constraint 3 (Human Oversight): Every external action requires approval or follows pre-approved patterns. Ambiguous actions are escalated. Constraint 4 (Task Completion): The agent executes its assigned task, nothing more. No scope expansion, no side projects.
content.benchmark: Exfiltration filter: 0 true positives missed in 10K test cases. Self-preservation: not applicable (no self-model). Human oversight: 100% of external actions logged. Task completion: 94% within scope, 6% minor scope adjustments.
content.caveat: No safety system is perfect. The constraints reduce risk but do not eliminate it. Unknown-unknowns (novel attack vectors) are always possible. Defense in depth.
